@@ -62,31 +62,43 @@ jobs:
             os: ubuntu-latest
             archive: tar.gz
             name: linux_amd64
+            can_run: true
+            linker: ""
           # Linux x64 (musl) — statically linked, no glibc dependency
           - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
             os: ubuntu-latest
             archive: tar.gz
             name: linux_musl_amd64
+            can_run: true
+            linker: ""
           # Linux ARM64 (gnu)
           - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
-            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
+            os: ubuntu-latest
             archive: tar.gz
             name: linux_arm64
+            can_run: false
+            linker: aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc
           # macOS Intel
           - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
             os: macos-15
             archive: tar.gz
             name: darwin_amd64
+            can_run: true
+            linker: ""
           # macOS Apple Silicon (native)
           - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
             os: macos-14
             archive: tar.gz
             name: darwin_arm64
+            can_run: true
+            linker: ""
           # Windows x64
-          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
-            os: windows-latest
+          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-gnu
+            os: ubuntu-latest
             archive: zip
             name: windows_amd64
+            can_run: false
+            linker: x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc
 
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
@@ -112,8 +124,30 @@ jobs:
         if: contains(matrix.target, 'musl')
         run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools
 
+      - name: Install cross toolchains
+        if: matrix.target == 'aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu' || matrix.target == 'x86_64-pc-windows-gnu'
+        run: |
+          sudo apt-get update
+          if [ "${{ matrix.target }}" = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu" ]; then
+            sudo apt-get install -y gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu libc6-dev-arm64-cross
+          elif [ "${{ matrix.target }}" = "x86_64-pc-windows-gnu" ]; then
+            sudo apt-get install -y mingw-w64 zip
+          fi
+
+      - name: Configure cross linker
+        if: matrix.linker != ''
+        run: |
+          case "${{ matrix.target }}" in
+            aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu)
+              echo "CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_UNKNOWN_LINUX_GNU_LINKER=${{ matrix.linker }}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
+              ;;
+            x86_64-pc-windows-gnu)
+              echo "CARGO_TARGET_X86_64_PC_WINDOWS_GNU_LINKER=${{ matrix.linker }}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
+              ;;
+          esac
+
       - name: Cache cargo
-        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@ad397744b0d591a723ab90405b7247fac0e6b8db  # v2
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@e18b497796c12c097a38f9edb9d0641fb99eee32  # v2.9.1
         with:
           key: release-${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-${{ matrix.target }}
 
@@ -123,12 +157,16 @@ jobs:
           VERGEN_GIT_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
           VERGEN_GIT_BRANCH: ${{ github.ref_name }}
 
-      - name: Verify binary version matches tag (Unix)
-        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
+      - name: Verify binary version matches tag
+        if: matrix.can_run
         run: |
           EXPECTED="${{ github.ref_name }}"
           EXPECTED="${EXPECTED#v}"  # strip leading v
-          RAW=$(./target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/br --version 2>&1)
+          BINARY="br"
+          if [ "${{ matrix.archive }}" = "zip" ]; then
+            BINARY="br.exe"
+          fi
+          RAW=$(./target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${BINARY} --version 2>&1)
           # clap outputs "br X.Y.Z"; extract the version token
           ACTUAL=$(echo "$RAW" | awk '{print $NF}')
           if [ "$ACTUAL" != "$EXPECTED" ]; then
@@ -137,33 +175,24 @@ jobs:
           fi
           echo "Version check passed: $ACTUAL == $EXPECTED"
 
-      - name: Verify binary version matches tag (Windows)
-        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
-        shell: pwsh
+      - name: Record cross-build verification strategy
+        if: ${{ !matrix.can_run }}
         run: |
-          $expected = "${{ github.ref_name }}" -replace '^v', ''
-          $raw = & ./target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/br.exe --version 2>&1 | Out-String
-          # clap outputs "br X.Y.Z"; extract the version token
-          $actual = ($raw.Trim() -split '\s+')[-1]
-          if ($actual -ne $expected) {
-            Write-Error "ERROR: Binary reports version '$actual' (from '$raw') but expected '$expected'"
-            exit 1
-          }
-          Write-Host "Version check passed: $actual == $expected"
+          echo "Cross-built target ${{ matrix.target }} cannot be executed on ${{ runner.os }}."
+          echo "Relying on tag/Cargo.toml preflight and successful link output instead."
 
-      - name: Create archive (Unix)
-        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
+      - name: Create archive (tar.gz)
+        if: matrix.archive == 'tar.gz'
         run: |
           cd target/${{ matrix.target }}/release
           tar -czvf ../../../br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.tar.gz br
           cd ../../..
 
-      - name: Create archive (Windows)
-        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
-        shell: pwsh
+      - name: Create archive (zip)
+        if: matrix.archive == 'zip'
         run: |
           cd target/${{ matrix.target }}/release
-          Compress-Archive -Path br.exe -DestinationPath ../../../br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.zip
+          zip -j ../../../br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.zip br.exe
           cd ../../..
 
       - name: Install minisign (Linux)
@@ -193,13 +222,6 @@ jobs:
         run: |
           shasum -a 256 br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.${{ matrix.archive }} > br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.${{ matrix.archive }}.sha256
 
-      - name: Generate checksum (Windows)
-        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
-        shell: pwsh
-        run: |
-          $hash = (Get-FileHash -Path "br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.${{ matrix.archive }}" -Algorithm SHA256).Hash.ToLower()
-          "$hash  br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.${{ matrix.archive }}" | Out-File -FilePath "br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.${{ matrix.archive }}.sha256" -Encoding utf8 -NoNewline
-
       - name: Verify checksum (Linux)
         if: runner.os == 'Linux'
         run: sha256sum -c br-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ matrix.name }}.${{ matrix.archive }}.sha256
@@ -250,7 +272,7 @@ jobs:
       - name: Validate required artifacts present
         run: |
           MISSING=()
-          for platform in linux_amd64 linux_musl_amd64 darwin_amd64 darwin_arm64; do
+          for platform in linux_amd64 linux_musl_amd64 linux_arm64 darwin_amd64 darwin_arm64 windows_amd64; do
             if ! ls artifacts/br-*-${platform}.* 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then
               MISSING+=("$platform")
             fi

@@ -15,6 +15,21 @@ This changelog is organized by capability rather than diff order. Each version s
 
 ---
 
+## [v0.1.33](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/releases/tag/v0.1.33) -- 2026-03-23 (Release)
+
+This release supersedes the partial `v0.1.32` fallback build by fixing release automation so `dsr` can produce installer-compatible assets deterministically.
+
+### Release and CI
+
+- **Rust cache pinning** was updated across all GitHub workflows to the current signed `Swatinem/rust-cache` v2.9.1 commit after the prior pinned SHA stopped resolving and broke fallback builds.
+- **Release builds now fail closed on missing artifacts**: Linux ARM64 and Windows AMD64 are treated as required release outputs instead of being silently omitted from a published release.
+- **Cross-platform fallback coverage improved** by moving Linux ARM64 and Windows AMD64 fallback builds onto Linux-based cross-compilation paths, reducing dependence on specialized remote runners for those targets.
+
+### Testing
+
+- **Single-issue graph rendering** now preserves DFS subtree order in plain output so dependents render contiguously instead of visually nesting under later siblings.
+- **List output regression coverage** now reflects the actual plain-output behavior for unknown custom statuses, keeping release validation aligned with user-visible CLI output.
+
 ## [v0.1.32](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/releases/tag/v0.1.32) -- 2026-03-23 (Release)
 
 This release extends cross-project routing coverage, hardens storage for frankensqlite compatibility, and tightens the release pipeline around version and installer correctness.

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "beads_rust"
-version = "0.1.32"
+version = "0.1.33"
 edition = "2024"
 rust-version = "1.88"
 description = "Agent-first issue tracker (SQLite + JSONL)"

@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@
 # To install from AUR: yay -S br-bin
 
 pkgname=br-bin
-pkgver=0.1.32
+pkgver=0.1.33
 pkgrel=1
 pkgdesc="Agent-first issue tracker (SQLite + JSONL)"
 arch=('x86_64' 'aarch64')

@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@ class Br < Formula
   desc "Agent-first issue tracker (SQLite + JSONL)"
   homepage "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust"
   license "MIT"
-  version "0.1.32"
+  version "0.1.33"
 
   on_macos do
     on_arm do
